action_items.action: RESOLVED — Sprint 169 publish hold LIFTED. Regular publishes flowing normally in #publish-gdc all day: GDC BE (08:00), NZ (08:59, 11:10), CA (09:31, 10:21), DE (12:33), RO (12:38). The morning hold is cleared. No PM action needed — monitor for re-occurrence.

ESCALATE NOW — GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign still Blocked (Day 15). Root cause now confirmed in ticket comments: Chloe Christie stated on April 15 'Blocked while awaiting feedback from Michael Butlin.' Butlin commented April 10: 'I can't get this to work.' Ciara's Solutions 02 prototype has been waiting on Butlin's response for 15 days. Mike must escalate directly to Michael Butlin today — this ticket cannot drift into another sprint without resolution.

PERFORMANCE ISSUE — Jessica Dordevic still has not made the Tinder Style design file changes Mike requested. Mike flagged this to Chloe Christie on April 30 at 12:39: 'Frustratingly Jess hasn't made any of the changes on the design file I asked her to make yesterday. I've asked her again.' Design brief re-confirmed: one simple card — swipe right or heart takes user to operator landing page after a match animation, no A/B test. Check whether Jessica has actioned this today. If not, a direct conversation is needed before sprint close.

PRIORITISE — GDCU-8935 World Cup Predictor Game (Miguel Migneco, Backlog) created today (11:08). This is the FTP WC game Mike explicitly flagged to Chloe on April 29: 'We need to prio anything WC related now — the FTP game, the Cosmo reskin and Group Bets.' Full end-to-end design: entry, predictions (group + knockout + outrights), leaderboard, private leagues, Daily Acca CTA, re-engagement, IUJ chip integration. Prize pool £10k. Confirm sprint placement with Chloe and ensure Miguel has context to start.

ENDORSE — GDCU-8893 Design System / Claude Design Guidelines POSTED by Ciara Carroll in #core-product on April 29 at 15:49. Comprehensive post: what Claude Design is, team and individual etiquette, GDC's built design system (3,000 components), org-level rules, and resources. Ciara's DM on April 29 at 17:58: 'Hoping the announcement came across well and we get feedback!' Mike should now publicly endorse in #core-product and amplify to #gdc, #language, and #ai-chatter as planned. Chloe also asked 'Are we happy to close the design system ticket?' on April 29 — confirm the close.

SIGN OFF — GDCU-8736 Learning Hub Imagery (Ciara Carroll, still In Product). Chloe flagged in sprint close message: 'I know there's some back and forth with the learning hub.' Ciara has shared two Figma prototype links in ticket comments and tagged Mike, Michael Butlin, Chloe, and Joshua Smith. Approve or provide specific feedback — this unblocks Carlos Blauth's GDCU-8733 dev work which remains In Progress.

SPRINT CLOSE — Chloe Christie is closing Sprint 169. Confirmed carry-overs (from #gdc-product-design-pod, April 30): GDCU-8879 (Brand Carousel Card, Jess), GDCU-8846 (Cosmo Spins Ad Campaign, Ciara), GDCU-8546 (Tinder Style, Jess — Chloe: 'will probably get that closed shortly'), GDCU-8736 (Learning Hub Imagery, Ciara), GDCU-8512 (Always On Betting Tips, Miguel — 'just waiting on the cards'). Confirm carry-over list and whether any items need re-prioritisation given the WC push.

NEW TICKET — GDCU-8947 Cosmo Cash Machine Images (Ciara Carroll, Backlog, created today at 13:07). Ciara posted in #gdc-product-design-pod: 'ticket added for the cosmo cash machine — can make cross-functional since Carlos ran a fix also?' Likely linked to WC Cosmo Spins reskin priority Mike flagged on April 29. Confirm scope, priority, and whether this enters the next sprint.

DESIGN DECISION — GDCU-8787 Carousel Banner Shortcode. Joshua Smith posted in #gdc-product-design-pod today (09:23): should the team re-use the Simple Banner shortcode (just add arrows to cycle through banners) or adopt the Free Games banner style (different layout — can't re-use shortcode)? Josh's recommendation is re-use for reduced editor complexity. Decision needed so design work can proceed after bank holidays.

CHECK — GDCU-8834 Cool Tones Update (Harden Thomas Wallace). Moved back to In Progress from In QA at 18:31 on April 29 — QA failure. No further status update visible in afternoon data. Confirm with Harden whether the QA issue has been resolved before sprint close.

CHECK — GDCU-8842 Genesis Content Layouts (Harden Thomas Wallace, still In Progress). Was flagged at 08:29 this morning alongside the Sprint 169 publish hold. Hold has since been lifted. Confirm whether this ticket completed and went live with the Sprint 169 release, or is carrying to the next sprint.

CANCELLED TODAY — GDCU-8901 Operator Images for Instagram Oplist (High, NZ) cancelled at 10:54. 20 operator logos were in scope for an Instagram oplist A/B test. No assignee was set. Confirm this cancellation is intentional and the Instagram oplist A/B test has been formally deprioritised.

CONFIRM CLOSE — GDCU-8813 Ace Navigation UI Audit (Miguel Migneco) no longer appears in the In Product queue this afternoon — likely approved. However, GDCU-8936 (Implement Redesign of Ace Navigation with Global Nav Bar) is now in Backlog with no assignee. Confirm with Chloe that the dev implementation ticket is lined up for the next sprint.

NEW JIRA — GDCU-8902 Sports Quiz Design System Pressure Test (Jessica Dordevic, In Progress, updated April 30 at 17:25). Using the Sports Quiz as a pressure test for the GDC Design System. Confirm this aligns with sprint scope and that Jessica has the bandwidth alongside GDCU-8546 and GDCU-8879.
Vic Dadson still on sick leave — expected back Tuesday 2026-05-05. World Cup Predictor game (GDCU-8935) and data engineering discussions will need her input. No action today unless cover is required.

CARRY FORWARD — GDCU-8903 Sports Quiz Hub UX pre-req ticket still not created. Dev ticket states it cannot be actioned until the UX/design handoff is complete and approved. Create or delegate before next sprint planning.

CARRY FORWARD — GDCU-8846 Cosmo Spins Spring Ad Campaign (Ciara Carroll, To Do). Carrying into next sprint per Chloe's sprint close. Creative direction from Mike still needed: Josh Kerr feedback was clearer message, less 'gaming' feel. May also link to WC Cosmo Spins reskin (GDCU-8947).

CARRY FORWARD — Gabriel Cornoiu's response to the stakeholder intake questions (April 23) still needs acknowledgement. Gab suggested adding: (1) SEO considerations and information architecture for new pages/hubs, and (2) which operator offer attributes should be displayed. Mike should reply to close the loop.

needs_input.context: Day 15 Blocked. Root cause confirmed in ticket: Chloe Christie's April 15 comment states 'Blocked while awaiting feedback from Michael Butlin.' Butlin commented April 10 he 'can't get this to work.' Joshua Smith has also added UX suggestions (surface 'our rating' on collapsed cards; auto-scroll on mobile expand). Mike needs to escalate directly to Michael Butlin — without his feedback, Ciara cannot finalise the Solutions 02 prototype and the ticket cannot close.

needs_input.summary: [GDC][UX] Share/Create Imagery for the Learning Hub
needs_input.context: Still In Product since April 28. Chloe noted 'some back and forth' in her sprint close message. Ciara has shared two Figma prototype links in ticket comments (April 16 and April 28) and tagged Mike, Michael Butlin, Chloe Christie, and Joshua Smith. Mike's approval is needed to unblock Carlos Blauth's GDCU-8733 Apply Feedback to Learning Hub dev ticket, which remains In Progress.
